title: planchonella assamica
intro: Planchonella assamica, also known as the Bintangor tree, is a plant species that belongs to the sapindaceae family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Planchonella assamica include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In terms of herbal preparations, Planchonella assamica is typically used in the form of a decoction, where the bark is boiled in water to create a medicinal tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
